scope: Viewport surface reconstruction only. The Wave/Bump layer appearance changes rendered normals but not mesh vertices; it is not a slicer or G-code toolpath.

capture.lightScaleInterpretation: Blender Area energy and Three.js RectAreaLight intensity are renderer-specific units. Blender's Medium High Contrast look clips this neutral filament almost entirely white. Increasing the browser light from 45 through 180 to 720 established an exposure ceiling: the remaining dark lines come from derivative bump filtering, not insufficient light.

interpretation: The authored geometry, material assignment, col field, and shared filament graph are recovered, and the aligned silhouette is near-identical. Blender's contrast transform clips 99.98% of the surface pixels white, while the WebGL derivative bump retains dark subpixel bands even after a 16x-to-64x exposure sweep. This measured material raster residual is documented rather than treated as mesh or G-code geometry.
